reasoning: Neither `container_perms_wf` nor `container_tree_wf` constrains `owned_endpoints`. The code comments explicitly state endpoints should "ONLY be referenced by threads in the subtree," yet no spec enforces cross-container disjointness.
`new_container_ensures` constrains tree-structural fields but never requires `owned_procs@ =~= Seq::empty()`. A freshly created container should logically have no processes.
`new_container_ensures` never constrains the scheduler. A freshly created container should have an empty scheduler since it has no threads yet.
`new_container_ensures` never requires `root_process == None`. A freshly created container with no processes should not already have a root process assigned.
Neither `container_perms_wf` nor `container_tree_wf` constrains `owned_cpus`. CPUs are exclusive hardware resources, so two containers owning the same CPU violates resource isolation.
